title: Oil Prices Will Remain High as Iran War Continues
summary: While officials look for ways to ease oil shocks, experts say higher prices will likely persist until traffic through the Strait of Hormuz returns.

title: Countries in Asia Try to Contain the Economic Fallout of the Mideast Conflict
summary: Across Asia, where countries are highly exposed to rising oil and gas costs and tightening supply, governments are acting to mitigate economic harm.

title: France Is Sending a Large Naval Force to the Middle East
summary: President Emmanuel Macron said the warships would help protect France’s allies in the region, and could be part of a force to escort ships through the Strait of Hormuz. He said the war could continue for “several days, maybe several weeks.”

title: Chaos on Mamdani’s Doorstep: ‘We’ve Never Had Anything Like This Here’
summary: For two hours, the streets around Gracie Mansion became the stage for a heated protest, reflecting a nation seething with angst and unease.

title: Homemade Bombs in ISIS-Inspired Attack Were Deadly, N.Y.P.D. Says
summary: Prosecutors say one of the two men who were arrested hoped the attack would be deadlier than the Boston Marathon bombing. Officials said they are seeing increasing signs of radicalization.

title: Alexander Brothers Found Guilty on All Counts in Sex-Trafficking Trial
summary: The verdict comes more than a month after the trial began in Federal District Court in Manhattan where the jury heard weeks of emotional and often graphic testimony.

title: 2 Teen Mariachi Musicians Released From ICE Detention
summary: Representative Joaquin Castro of Texas led a delegation of Democrats to a South Texas detention center to press for the release of the brothers and their family.

title: Locals in Other States Prepare for ICE With Lots of Rumors and Little Information
summary: After the sprawling and chaotic federal immigration crackdown in Minnesota, jurisdictions in other states have been bracing for a range of scenarios.

title: Trump Threatens to Crowd Out Republicans’ Midterm Message
summary: As the G.O.P. gathered in Miami for a party retreat where lawmakers hoped to focus on the economy, the president threatened to block his own party’s legislative agenda.

title: In Scathing Ruling, Judge Says 3 Trump Prosecutors Are in Unlawful Roles
summary: The ruling found that the three-person leadership team in New Jersey’s federal prosecutor’s office was illegal, and sharply questioned President Trump’s priorities.

title: His Mother Vanished When He Was 14. 33 Years Later, He Found Her.
summary: Antonio Wiley’s mother, Anita, vanished during a time when her hometown Detroit was wracked by drugs and crime. It took three decades and an extraordinary effort by a missing persons detective to locate her.

title: O.S.U. President Walter Carter Jr. Resigns Over ‘Inappropriate Relationship’
summary: The university said it was investigating a company owned by a podcaster after the president, Walter Carter Jr., said he had “made a mistake.”

title: Casey Wasserman Agency Removes His Name From Company in Epstein Fallout
summary: The sports and marketing agency is contending with the fallout from the Epstein files, and it is continuing to look for a buyer.

title: Epstein’s Remote Zorro Ranch Is Searched by New Mexico Investigators
summary: Some of the disgraced financier’s victims have said they were trafficked at the property south of Santa Fe, where the nearest neighbors are miles away.

title: Billionaires Made 19% of Federal Election Campaign Contributions in 2024
summary: Billionaires made 19 percent of all reported federal campaign contributions in 2024, a Times analysis shows, and even more in some local elections. Wealthy donors are reaping the rewards.

title: Alexander Butterfield, Who Revealed Nixon Tapes in Watergate Scandal, Dies at 99
summary: “There is tape in the Oval Office,” said Mr. Butterfield, a former White House aide, in testimony that rocked the Watergate hearings and led to the president’s resignation.

title: A Night Light in the Sky? Reflect Orbital Wants to Launch a Big Space Mirror.
summary: The company is seeking F.C.C. approval to test an idea to reflect sunlight to Earth at night, possibly powering solar panels. Critics say it could be bad for people and wildlife.

title: For Longtime Kennedy Center Patrons, a ‘Lifeline’ Has Been Cut
summary: As President Trump prepares to close Washington’s premier performing arts venue for two years, loyal patrons wonder where they’ll get their cultural fix.

title: After Kennedy Center Exit, Washington National Opera Returns
summary: Washington National Opera managed to resume performances within two months of its abrupt departure. But there are still challenges ahead.

title: Bombing Iran to Rubble Won’t Give It Life
summary: Only when the U.S.-Israeli bombing of Iran comes to a halt, without destroying the state, can the Iranian people sort out their fate.

title: They Feel Bugs Inside Them. Doctors Don’t Know What They Are Either.
summary: Delusional infestation is one of the darkest embodiments of medical uncertainty.

title: Iran War Sends Tremors Through the Global Economy
summary: Also, a giant mirror could soon be sent into space. Here’s the latest at the end of Monday.

title: Meteorite Crashes Through Roof in Germany After Fiery Light Show
summary: The fireball from space was spotted by a network of sky-watching cameras in Belgium, France, Luxembourg, the Netherlands and Germany.

title: Monti Rock III, Gleefully Untalented ‘Tonight Show’ Favorite, Dies at 86
summary: He couldn’t sing, dance or tell funny stories. But Johnny Carson loved him and his persona: a D-list star clinging to celebrity.

title: An Amish Avatar and an A.I. Monk Are Pitching Supplements on Social Media
summary: Influencers have long hawked supplements on platforms like Instagram and TikTok. Increasingly, the influencers are fake.

title: Lawmakers Pressure Hochul to Raise Taxes in New York
summary: The State Assembly and Senate are each calling for the state to raise taxes, piggybacking on a push by Mayor Zohran Mamdani of New York City.
